description:
The Wazuh indexer contains security alerts spanning March 10-15 in indices matching wazuh-alerts-4.x-2025.03.*. A multi-stage intrusion occurred during this period, hidden among routine operational alerts from two monitored hosts.

Credentials: Dashboard - admin / SecretPassword (https://localhost). Manager API - wazuh-wui / MyS3cr37P450r.*- (https://localhost:55000). Indexer - admin / SecretPassword (https://localhost:9200).

Using the Wazuh dashboard or API, investigate the alerts to reconstruct the complete attack chain. Save your findings to ~/apt_report.json with these keys:

- attacker_ip (string): the external attacker IP address
- compromised_account (string): the first user account compromised
- compromised_host (string): the first host breached
- lateral_movement_target (string): the internal host the attacker pivoted to
- privilege_escalation_method (string): how root access was obtained
- persistence_mechanism (string): how the attacker maintained long-term access
- attack_timeline (array): chronological objects with timestamp, host, and event string fields for each attack stage

Then, in the wazuh-wazuh.manager-1 container, create correlated detection rules in /var/ossec/etc/rules/local_rules.xml using if_matched_sid to chain at least two attack stages. Restart the Wazuh manager and confirm your rules load successfully by querying the manager API.

ground_truth.privilege_escalation_method: sudo su to root, created backdoor user sysadm1n with UID 0
